title: Binlog Table Maintenance Handling
description: Processing DDL/table maintenance statements (OPTIMIZE, ANALYZE, REPAIR) from the binlog stream. These statements are read from the binlog but are explicitly filtered out and never stored in schema history or dispatched as change events.
activity_flow: START: MySQL/MariaDB executes ANALYZE TABLE / OPTIMIZE TABLE / REPAIR TABLE
Binlog captures statement as QUERY event
BinlogStreamingChangeEventSource: EventType.QUERY handler invokes handleQueryEvent(partition, offsetContext, event)
DECISION: sql starts with 'XA '? → ignore, return
→ TRUE (matches ANALYZE/OPTIMIZE/REPAIR regex): log DEBUG 'DDL filtered out'; return [EARLY EXIT - no further processing]
→ FALSE: continue processing as normal DDL
schema.parseStreamingDdl(partition, sql, database, offsetContext, eventTime) → List<SchemaChangeEvent>
For ANALYZE/OPTIMIZE/REPAIR: parser produces NO DdlParserListener events (0 table events)
ddlChanges.isEmpty() == true → emits SchemaChangeEvent.ofDatabase() as fallback
DECISION: event type == TRUNCATE AND tableId != null? → dispatchDataChangeEvent(TRUNCATE)

filter_logic.result_when_filtered: LOGGER.debug("DDL '{}' was filtered out of processing"); return — no SchemaChangeEvent emitted, no Kafka record produced

parser_behavior.result: tables.size()==1, listener.total()==1 — ANALYZE/OPTIMIZE/REPAIR produce zero DdlParserListener events
parser_behavior.conclusion: DDL parser silently ignores maintenance statements; they generate no schema-change listener events

test_class_hierarchy: BinlogTableMaintenanceStatementsIT<C extends SourceConnector> extends AbstractBinlogConnectorIT<C> [abstract, package: io.debezium.connector.binlog]

ddl_operations_in_sql: CREATE TABLE dbz_253_table_maintenance_test (id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PK, other INT)

key_test_method.name: shouldConsumeAllEventsFromDatabaseUsingBinlogAndNoSnapshot
assertions: records for serverName topic == 2 (CREATE DB + CREATE TABLE only)
key_test_method.comment: numTableMaintenanceStatements = 0 explicitly — ANALYZE/OPTIMIZE/REPAIR not stored in schema history

schema_history_filter_confirmation.test: execute ANALYZE TABLE + OPTIMIZE TABLE after CREATE TABLE
schema_history_filter_confirmation.result: consumeRecordsByTopic(1) receives only CREATE TABLE; ddl field contains 'CREATE TABLE employees'; ANALYZE/OPTIMIZE produce no records

important_notes: ANALYZE/OPTIMIZE/REPAIR are filtered at two levels: (1) DDL filter regex in SchemaHistory rejects them before parseStreamingDdl(), (2) DdlParser produces zero events even if parsed directly
Filter is applied in handleQueryEvent() via schema.ddlFilter().test(sql) — short-circuits entire processing pipeline
SnapshotMode.NEVER means connector reads only binlog — no initial snapshot; only binlog events (CREATE DB, CREATE TABLE) are captured
MariaDB supports 'SET STATEMENT ... FOR ANALYZE/OPTIMIZE/REPAIR' syntax — regex handles this variant
